title: PM Modi Visits Seychelles as Guest of Honour at Golden Jubilee National Day Celebrations
summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi travelled to Seychelles to attend the golden jubilee (50th anniversary) of the island nation's National Day as Guest of Honour. The visit underscores India's deepening ties with Indian Ocean island nations under its 'Neighbourhood First' and SAGAR (Security and Growth for All in the Region) policy. During the visit, Modi was also scheduled to meet Jonathan, widely recognised as the world's oldest living land animal — a giant tortoise aged approximately 192 years.
exam_angle: India's diplomatic outreach to Indian Ocean island states is a recurring IR theme; connects to SAGAR doctrine, blue economy, and India's maritime neighbourhood strategy.
key_facts: Occasion: Golden Jubilee (50th anniversary) of Seychelles' National Day
PM Modi attended as Guest of Honour
Connects to India's SAGAR policy and Indian Ocean outreach
Jonathan the tortoise (~192 years old) — world's oldest living land animal — met during visit

title: US Secretary of State Rubio Confirms Trump Likely to Visit India in Early 2027; India-US Trade Deal Near Finalisation
summary: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio stated that President Donald Trump is likely to visit India in early 2027, describing his relationship with PM Modi as excellent. Rubio also indicated that an India-US bilateral trade deal is close to finalisation, with only a 'handful of issues' remaining. US Ambassador to India Sergio Gor separately noted that H-1B visa restrictions were not India-specific. The announcements signal a significant upswing in India-US strategic and economic engagement.
exam_angle: India-US bilateral trade relations, BTA negotiations, and high-level diplomatic visits are standard UPSC IR topics; connects to Quad, Indo-Pacific strategy, and technology partnership.
key_facts: Trump India visit expected: early 2027 (per Rubio)
India-US trade deal described as near-finalisation
US envoy: H-1B curbs not India-specific
Marco Rubio is US Secretary of State; Sergio Gor is US Ambassador to India
source: The Economic Times / The Hindu / NDTV

title: India Launches 'Operation Amistad' — Sends Humanitarian Relief to Earthquake-Hit Venezuela
summary: India dispatched relief material to Venezuela under 'Operation Amistad' (meaning 'friendship' in Spanish) following a devastating earthquake that killed over 920 people. The operation demonstrates India's Humanitarian Assistance and Disaster Relief (HADR) capabilities and its broader global outreach. Venezuela's president expressed confidence in rescue operations as the 72-hour rescue window neared its end.
exam_angle: India's HADR missions are a key IR and defence topic; 'Operation Amistad' can be asked as an example of India's soft power and disaster diplomacy in Latin America.
key_facts: Operation name: Operation Amistad ('friendship')
Triggered by: Venezuela earthquake (death toll 920+)
India provided: humanitarian relief material
Demonstrates India's HADR capability beyond immediate neighbourhood

title: India-New Zealand Free Trade Agreement Under Discussion as Modern Trade Partnership
summary: Discussions around an India-New Zealand Free Trade Agreement (FTA) have been highlighted as a potential 'modern trade partnership', covering goods, services, and digital trade. The FTA is part of India's broader strategy of securing bilateral trade agreements with key partners amid global trade realignments. India has been actively pursuing FTAs with the UK, EU, and several other nations in recent years.
exam_angle: India's FTA strategy is a major GS-3 economy and GS-2 IR topic; the New Zealand FTA adds to the list of ongoing negotiations students must track alongside UK-FTA, EU-FTA, and GCC-FTA.
key_facts: India-New Zealand FTA: described as a 'modern trade partnership'
Covers goods, services, and digital trade components
Part of India's active bilateral FTA strategy
Other ongoing FTAs: UK, EU, Canada, GCC

title: India Starts World's First Hydrogen Production Facility Using Nuclear Technology
summary: India has commissioned what is being described as the world's first hydrogen production facility that uses nuclear technology, marking a significant milestone in clean energy. The facility leverages high-temperature nuclear reactors to produce hydrogen through thermochemical or electrolysis processes, offering a carbon-free hydrogen production pathway. This aligns with India's National Green Hydrogen Mission and its net-zero commitments.
exam_angle: Nuclear energy and green hydrogen are high-priority GS-3 Sci-Tech and environment topics; this development directly connects to India's National Green Hydrogen Mission (NGHM) and energy security.
key_facts: Claimed to be: world's first nuclear-technology-based hydrogen production facility
Technology: nuclear energy used for clean hydrogen production
Connects to: National Green Hydrogen Mission (NGHM)
Significance: carbon-free hydrogen pathway for India's energy transition

title: Indian Navy's Eastern Fleet Ships Arrive at Sattahip, Thailand for Port Visit
summary: Three ships of the Indian Navy's Eastern Fleet — INS Udaygiri, INS Kavaratti, and INS Shakti — arrived at Sattahip Naval Base in Thailand. Such port visits are part of India's naval diplomacy under the SAGAR framework and strengthen maritime partnerships in the Indo-Pacific region. The visit enhances defence cooperation and interoperability between Indian and Thai naval forces.
exam_angle: Indian Navy port visits to Indo-Pacific nations are standard UPSC defence and IR questions; INS Udaygiri (destroyer) and INS Kavaratti (ASW corvette) are frequently asked warship names.
key_facts: Ships: INS Udaygiri, INS Kavaratti, INS Shakti
Fleet: Eastern Fleet
Port visited: Sattahip Naval Base, Thailand
Context: naval diplomacy under SAGAR / Indo-Pacific engagement

title: Anthropic's Mythos AI Model Receives Limited US Government Approval for Broader Use
summary: The US government approved limited broader access to Anthropic's advanced AI model 'Mythos 5' after initially blocking its release over national security concerns. The Trump administration granted the approval with security safeguards in place, following Anthropic briefing the government on the model's capabilities. Separately, OpenAI announced new AI models named Sol, Terra, and Luna, with the US government restricting their wide release due to security implications.
exam_angle: AI governance and national security dimensions of frontier AI models are emerging Sci-Tech and IR topics for UPSC; connects to AI regulation, export controls, and responsible AI deployment.
key_facts: Anthropic's AI model: Mythos 5 — received limited US approval after national security review
OpenAI new models announced: Sol, Terra, Luna — US blocked wide release
Issue: national security concerns over powerful frontier AI models
Trend: governments increasingly regulating export and deployment of advanced AI
source: Business Standard / India Today / The Hindu

title: Kotak Mahindra Bank MD & CEO Ashok Vaswani to Step Down at Year-End
summary: Ashok Vaswani, the Managing Director and Chief Executive Officer of Kotak Mahindra Bank, announced he will step down at the end of his current term in December 2026 for personal reasons. The bank has begun the search for his successor. Vaswani had taken charge of the private sector bank in January 2024, succeeding founder Uday Kotak who stepped down as CEO.
exam_angle: Leadership changes at major private sector banks are relevant for economy and banking awareness; RBI's 'fit and proper' norms for bank CEOs and succession planning at systemically important banks are standard exam topics.
key_facts: Kotak Mahindra Bank CEO: Ashok Vaswani stepping down end of 2026
Reason: personal
Vaswani joined: January 2024 (succeeded founder Uday Kotak)
Kotak Mahindra Bank: one of India's largest private sector banks

title: Maharashtra Teacher Eligibility Test (TET) 2026 Postponed After Paper Leak Suspicion
summary: The Maharashtra Teacher Eligibility Test (TET) 2026 was postponed just one day before the scheduled examination date following suspicion of a paper leak originating from Bhiwandi. The incident adds to a series of high-profile examination paper leak controversies in India. The Maharashtra government faced sharp criticism, with opposition parties and educators questioning the integrity of public examinations.
exam_angle: Examination integrity, paper leak prevention mechanisms, and the role of state PSC/examination boards are polity and governance topics; connects to the Public Examinations (Prevention of Unfair Means) Act 2024 enacted by Parliament.
key_facts: Exam postponed: Maharashtra TET 2026
Reason: suspicion of paper leak (source traced to Bhiwandi)
Timing: postponed one day before scheduled exam
Central law context: Public Examinations (Prevention of Unfair Means) Act, 2024

title: Haryana IAS Officer Suspended After CBI Arrest in Rs 657-Crore IDFC Bank Scam
summary: The Haryana government suspended an IAS officer following their arrest by the Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI) in connection with a Rs 657-crore scam involving IDFC Bank. The case relates to alleged financial fraud and misuse of public funds. The suspension under All India Services (Discipline and Appeal) Rules follows standard protocol when an AIS officer is placed under arrest.
exam_angle: IAS officer accountability, All India Services rules, CBI jurisdiction over public servants, and anti-corruption mechanisms are standard GS-2 polity/governance topics.
key_facts: Arrested by: CBI
Scam amount: Rs 657 crore (IDFC Bank-related)
Action taken: suspension by Haryana government
Applicable rules: All India Services (Discipline and Appeal) Rules

title: India's Gaza Policy Debate: BJP-Congress Exchange as India Maintains Balanced Stand
summary: The BJP defended India's policy on the Gaza conflict as 'balanced', while Congress accused the government of playing vote-bank politics. India has traditionally maintained a two-state solution stance and called for cessation of hostilities, while also deepening its strategic ties with Israel. Simultaneously, Mirwaiz Umar Farooq, the chief religious leader of Kashmir, called for direct India-Pakistan talks.
exam_angle: India's West Asia policy — the Israel-Palestine question and the two-state solution — is a recurring UPSC IR topic; India's historical non-alignment-derived stance of supporting Palestine while maintaining Israel ties is frequently examined.
key_facts: India's stated position: balanced, supports two-state solution
BJP characterisation: Congress doing vote-bank politics on Gaza
India's Israel ties: deepened post-2017 (PM Modi's historic Israel visit)
Mirwaiz Umar Farooq: called for direct India-Pakistan dialogue

title: Oil Tanker Struck in Strait of Hormuz Amid Iran-US Military Exchange
summary: An oil tanker was struck by an 'unknown projectile' in the strategically critical Strait of Hormuz as Iran and the United States exchanged military strikes. Iran condemned the US strikes as a 'blatant violation of the peace deal'. The Strait of Hormuz is the world's most important oil chokepoint through which approximately 20% of global oil trade passes, making any conflict there highly significant for global energy markets including India.
exam_angle: The Strait of Hormuz as a global chokepoint, Iran-US tensions, and their impact on India's energy security and crude oil imports are critical GS-2 IR and GS-3 Economy topics.
key_facts: Location: Strait of Hormuz — world's most critical oil chokepoint (~20% global oil trade)
Incident: oil tanker hit by unknown projectile
Context: Iran-US military exchange
Iran's statement: US strikes are 'blatant violation of peace deal'

title: NEP 2020 and India's Education Crisis: Implementation Challenges Examined
summary: A detailed analysis by Frontline Magazine examined the implementation challenges of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 and India's broader education crisis. NEP 2020 envisions a transformation from rote learning to competency-based education, multidisciplinary approach, a 5+3+3+4 school structure, and a target of 50% Gross Enrolment Ratio in higher education by 2035. Issues such as teacher shortages, infrastructure gaps, and regional disparities in implementation have been highlighted.
exam_angle: NEP 2020 is one of the most frequently asked governance/education topics in UPSC GS-2; key provisions, pillars, and implementation gaps are must-know for aspirants.
key_facts: NEP 2020: approved by Union Cabinet in July 2020
Structural change: 10+2 replaced by 5+3+3+4 curricular framework
GER target: 50% in higher education by 2035 (from ~27% currently)
Key pillars: mother tongue as medium of instruction (till Class 5), multidisciplinary education, vocational training from Class 6

title: Qatar Repatriates All 12 Bodies of Indians Killed in Ras Laffan Gas Facility Fire
summary: Qatar completed the repatriation of all 12 bodies of Indian nationals killed in a fire at the Ras Laffan industrial complex, home to some of the world's largest liquefied natural gas (LNG) facilities. All 12 bodies have now been brought back to India. Ras Laffan Industrial City in Qatar is one of the world's largest concentrations of energy infrastructure, and India is a major buyer of Qatari LNG.
exam_angle: Indian diaspora welfare, India-Qatar bilateral relations (India is Qatar's largest source of migrant labour), and Ras Laffan as a strategic LNG hub are relevant IR and economy angles.
key_facts: Incident: fire at Ras Laffan Industrial Complex, Qatar
Casualties: 12 Indian nationals killed
All 12 bodies repatriated to India
Ras Laffan: one of world's largest LNG production hubs; Qatar is major LNG supplier to India
source: The New Indian Express / India Today

title: Hyderabad Names Road After US President Trump; Trump Thanks India
summary: A road in Hyderabad was named after US President Donald Trump, prompting Trump to publicly thank India and call it an 'unprecedented honour'. The gesture reflects the warm personal rapport between PM Modi and President Trump, which has been emphasised in diplomatic communication. Such symbolic acts of diplomatic goodwill, while ceremonial, signal the direction of bilateral relations.
exam_angle: India-US bilateral diplomacy and the role of soft power gestures in foreign policy are GS-2 IR topics; this can be used as an example of people-to-people diplomacy.
key_facts: Road named after: US President Donald Trump
City: Hyderabad
Trump's response: called it 'unprecedented honour', publicly thanked India
Context: warm Modi-Trump bilateral relationship ahead of potential 2027 state visit

title: MDMK Quits DMK-Led Alliance in Tamil Nadu After 9 Years
summary: The Marumalarchi Dravida Munnetra Kazhagam (MDMK), led by Vaiko, quit the DMK-led alliance in Tamil Nadu after nine years, citing political differences. This is a setback for Chief Minister MK Stalin's DMK ahead of future state elections. Tamil Nadu coalition politics is significant given the state's complex multi-party system and the role of regional parties in national politics.
exam_angle: Coalition dynamics, regional party politics, and the role of Tamil Nadu's multi-party system are polity topics; MDMK-DMK relations and Tamil Nadu's political landscape are frequently examined in State PSC exams.
key_facts: MDMK: Marumalarchi Dravida Munnetra Kazhagam, led by Vaiko
Alliance quit after: 9 years with DMK
Tamil Nadu CM: MK Stalin (DMK)
Significance: potential impact on INDIA bloc and Tamil Nadu electoral arithmetic
